ArchitectureNotes.objective: Polza.AI Dashboard — мониторинг генераций + LLM-ассистированный анализ промптов сотрудников. После simplify-ui refactor фокус сместился с per-employee агрегации на per-generation AI-анализ в детальной модалке.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Не строить отдельное приложение — расширяем существующий Flask + SPA. Не заменять Polza.AI API как proxy для LLM — используем Anthropic напрямую отдельным ключом.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Rate limit Anthropic при bulk-analyze — mitigated via concurrency=3 + progressive UI.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: LLM может некорректно классифицировать рабочее/личное — prompt явно требует is_work + risk_flags; human review через модалку.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Только 25% генераций имеют session_id (sparse Polza.AI metadata). Session-level features — graceful fallback: sub-блок в модалке только при наличии session_id.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-4: Anthropic prompt-caching имеет минимум 2048 токенов для Haiku 4.5; текущие system-промпты короче — кеш молча не применяется. Экономия обеспечивается persistent DB-кешем вместо prompt-cache.
ArchitectureNotes.arch-dual-provider: Phase-4 introduces dual LLM provider: Anthropic (Cloud) via native Messages API vs Ollama (On-Prem) via native /api/chat. Runtime switching via _provider_state. Ollama native API with think:false gives 2-5s response; OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions does NOT support think parameter. /no_think in prompt doesn't work — must use the API parameter.
ArchitectureNotes.arch-vector-storage: Phase-4 adds Qdrant vector DB (separate container polza-qdrant on port 6335). Every summarize call now runs LLM + embedding in PARALLEL via ThreadPoolExecutor. Vectors are 768-dim cosine (nomic-embed-text-v2-moe). Payload includes generation_id, topic, session_id, key_name, model, created_at. Enables future semantic search across all analyzed generations.

contract.purpose: Minimal entrypoint: creates Flask app from config, registers all route blueprints, starts background workers, runs server. Only contains main() function.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Entrypoint: init Flask app, register routes, init DB, start workers, run server

contract.purpose: Flask app factory, global state, environment variable loading, shared helper functions
LLM provider implementations: Anthropic, Ollama, OpenRouter. Prompt template and dispatch logic.

target.source: providers/__init__.py, providers/anthropic.py, providers/ollama.py, providers/openrouter.py, providers/prompt.py, providers/dispatcher.py

contract.purpose: Vector embedding generation and Qdrant storage
Background worker threads for analyze-all, auto-analyze, and session summarization
Flask route blueprints covering all HTTP endpoints

target.source: routes/__init__.py, routes/misc.py, routes/generations.py, routes/summarize.py, routes/sessions.py, routes/analyze.py, routes/employee.py, routes/provider.py, routes/keys.py, routes/sync.py, routes/proxy.py

contract.purpose: SQLAlchemy ORM: api_keys + generations + session_summaries + generation_summaries tables in PostgreSQL

fn-init_db.PURPOSE: Create all tables on startup via Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

fn-to_dict.PURPOSE: Serialize ORM objects to API-compatible JSON

contract.purpose: Background thread: incremental sync every 5 min + session_id enrichment
Background thread: enrich session_id/device_id for historical records
SPA dashboard after simplify-ui refactor: table with compact AI-cell, charts, sidebar, detail modal with embedded AI block, topic-grouping tab, backfill indicator
Parse API keys from Excel paste, .env, free text
SQLAlchemy model + CRUD for session_summaries table — caches LLM summaries per session

fn-summary_get_or_none.PURPOSE: Get summary by session_id, return None if not cached

fn-summary_upsert.PURPOSE: INSERT or UPDATE summary for a session_id

fn-summary_list_by_key.PURPOSE: List summaries for an employee within date range

contract.purpose: LLM-powered session summarization via Anthropic native API (Claude Haiku 4.5) with prompt-caching enabled

fn-summarize_single_session.PURPOSE: Summarize single session — returns from cache or calls LLM

contract.purpose: Rule-based anomaly detection. Code remains but UI consumer removed in simplify-ui refactor.

fn-detect.PURPOSE: Analyze employee records, return anomaly list

notes.note-1: Anomaly concept partially migrated into AI risk_flags returned by M-LLM-GEN-SUMMARIZE (personal, sensitive, high_cost, unusual_model).

contract.purpose: SQL aggregation endpoints per employee. No UI consumer after refactor; kept for potential future re-use.
Removed in simplify-ui refactor (2026-04-21). Replaced by in-modal AI analysis and 'По темам AI' tab.

target.source: (removed — no source files now consume this interface)

notes.note-1: Functionality intentionally cut because per-employee drill-down added complexity without replacing the primary investigation flow (which is: pick a suspicious row → see what the user did). The new flow satisfies UC-002 via detail modal + AI block.

contract.purpose: Persistent per-generation LLM cache — 100% cost savings on repeated views and prefetch on table render
contract.scope: CREATE TABLE generation_summaries, upsert, batch read by IDs, single read, delete
contract.inputs: generation_id, summary, topic, is_work, project_guess, risk_flags, usage metrics
contract.outputs: GenerationSummary ORM objects, JSON dicts with full usage info

export-GenerationSummary.PURPOSE: ORM model: generation_id PK + all summary + usage columns

fn-gen_summary_get_or_none.PURPOSE: Fetch single cached summary or None

fn-gen_summary_upsert.PURPOSE: INSERT or UPDATE with full usage metrics

fn-gen_summary_delete.PURPOSE: Remove cache entry to force regeneration

notes.note-1: Added alongside ApiKey, Generation, SessionSummary. init_db creates table via create_all.
notes.note-2: Table stores cache_creation_tokens, cache_read_tokens, input_tokens, output_tokens, llm_cost for observability — lets the UI show full economics in the modal meta line.

contract.purpose: Direct Anthropic Messages API — NOT proxied. Own key/URL/model in .env. Supports prompt caching on system block.
contract.scope: Build request payload, call API, parse response, compute cost, handle errors
contract.outputs: parsed LLM JSON, usage dict (input/output/cache_w/cache_r tokens, cost_usd)

errors.error: Anthropic returned non-200 — body logged, raises ValueError
Response has empty content[] — raises ValueError
LLM returned non-JSON despite strict prompt — caller catches JSONDecodeError

fn-llm_call_summarize.PURPOSE: Build payload with cache_control, call API, parse + compute cost. Helper shared by M-LLM-GEN-SUMMARIZE and M-SESSION-SUMMARIZER.

notes.note-1: Format quirks learned: (1) system is TOP-LEVEL array, not a message; (2) response is content[].text not choices[0].message.content; (3) headers are x-api-key + anthropic-version, NOT Bearer; (4) 404 body often arrives with status 401-looking wrappers — always read response.text.
notes.note-2: Pricing per 1M tokens (Haiku 4.5): input $1, output $5, cache_write $1.25, cache_read $0.10.
notes.note-3: Prompt cache minimum for Haiku 4.5 is 2048 tokens; short prompts silently bypass caching.

contract.purpose: Per-generation AI analysis: action + context + project + tech + risk flags. Manual trigger only. DB cache first.
contract.scope: Endpoints for single summarize, batch prefetch, cache delete. Prompt engineering. Cost accounting.
contract.outputs: JSON: {summary, topic, isWork, projectGuess, riskFlags, llmModel, llmCost, usage metrics, cached}

errors.error: Polza log has no extractable text — returns empty-placeholder result without calling LLM
LLM API failure — returns error JSON with details, non-500 to keep UI responsive
DB write failed — non-fatal, response still returned

fn-extract_user_text_from_log.PURPOSE: Helper: concatenate user-role messages (fallback any-role), cap 4000 chars

notes.note-1: Strict JSON-output prompt asks for 6-8 sentence detailed summary with action, context, technologies, goal. Schema: summary, topic, is_work, project_guess, risk_flags[]. max_tokens=900 (was 600).
notes.note-2: risk_flags values: personal, off_hours, unusual_model, high_cost, sensitive.
notes.note-3: Cost ~$0.002 per first call (Anthropic). Repeat calls from DB cache are $0 and ~5-10ms response.
notes.note-4: Phase-4: Now runs LLM call + embedding in PARALLEL via ThreadPoolExecutor. Stores result in both PostgreSQL (generation_summaries) and Qdrant (Polza_user_logs).

contract.purpose: Abstract LLM calls to support three providers: Anthropic (Cloud), Ollama (On-Prem), and OpenRouter (Cloud Free). Runtime provider switching via _provider_state.
contract.scope: Provider dispatch, Anthropic native call, Ollama native /api/chat call, OpenRouter OpenAI-compatible call, JSON parsing for Qwen output

errors.error: Selected provider fails to connect — fallback info in response
Qwen returns non-JSON or wraps in thinking tags — _parse_llm_json extracts JSON robustly

fn-_llm_call_anthropic.PURPOSE: Refactored Anthropic native Messages API call — original _llm_call_summarize code

fn-_llm_call_ollama.PURPOSE: New Ollama native /api/chat with think:false parameter — 2-5s response

fn-_llm_call_openrouter.PURPOSE: New OpenRouter OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions call — free Gemma 4 31B model

fn-_llm_call_summarize.PURPOSE: Dispatcher: selects provider based on _provider_state, calls appropriate handler

fn-_parse_llm_json.PURPOSE: Robust JSON extraction from Qwen output — handles <think> tags, markdown wrapping, partial JSON

fn-api_provider_config.PURPOSE: GET /api/provider/config — returns current provider, available providers, model info

fn-api_provider_set.PURPOSE: POST /api/provider/set — body: {provider: anthropic|ollama|openrouter}. Switches _provider_state at runtime

notes.note-1: Ollama native /api/chat with {"think": false} gives 2-5s response. OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions does NOT support the think parameter — must use native API.
notes.note-2: /no_think in prompt text doesn't work — must use the API-level think:false parameter.
notes.note-3: Qwen output may include <think>...</think> wrapper or markdown fences — _parse_llm_json strips these.
notes.note-4: OpenRouter uses OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions with Bearer auth. Model: google/gemma-4-31b-it:free. Cost: $0 (free tier). Response parsed from choices[0].message.content via _parse_llm_json.
notes.note-5: OPENROUTER_MODELS registry lists available models (Nemotron 31B, Gemma 4 31B). _provider_state.openrouter_model stores selected model, switchable via /api/provider/set {openrouterModel: "..."}.

contract.purpose: Generate 768-dim vector embeddings via Ollama nomic-embed-text-v2-moe model. Runs in parallel with LLM call.
contract.inputs: text string (user prompt from generation log)

fn-_embed_text.PURPOSE: Call Ollama /api/embed with nomic-embed-text-v2-moe, return embedding vector

notes.note-1: Runs via ThreadPoolExecutor in parallel with LLM call — does not add latency to summarize flow.
notes.note-2: nomic-embed-text-v2-moe produces 768-dim vectors. Qdrant collection configured for cosine similarity.

contract.purpose: Qdrant vector DB integration — store embedding vectors with generation metadata payload.
contract.scope: Lazy client init, collection creation, point upsert with payload
contract.inputs: generation_id, embedding vector, metadata (topic, session_id, key_name, model, created_at)

fn-_get_qdrant_client.PURPOSE: Lazy init Qdrant client with graceful fallback if qdrant_client not installed or QDRANT_ENABLED=false

fn-_qdrant_ensure_collection.PURPOSE: Create Polza_user_logs collection (768-dim, cosine) if not exists

fn-_qdrant_upsert.PURPOSE: Store vector + payload for a generation_id. Payload: generation_id, topic, session_id, key_name, model, created_at

notes.note-1: Separate Qdrant container polza-qdrant on VM port 6335 (not shared with socraticode-qdrant).
notes.note-2: Collection: Polza_user_logs, 768-dim cosine vectors. Lazy init — no error if qdrant_client not installed.

contract.purpose: Sidebar radio buttons for runtime LLM provider switching: On-Prem (Qwen) vs Cloud (Haiku) vs Cloud Free (Gemma 4 31B).
contract.scope: Provider radio, info labels, toggle handler, updated AI section with provider badge
contract.outputs: POST /api/provider/set, visual feedback with model/cost info

fn-loadProviderConfig.PURPOSE: GET /api/provider/config on page load — populate radio state and info labels

fn-setProvider.PURPOSE: POST /api/provider/set — switch provider, update UI labels

fn-renderAiSection_update.PURPOSE: Updated to show provider label (🏠/☁️) and vector badge (🔵 Embedded) in AI block

notes.note-1: Radio options: On-Prem (Qwen) — free, ~2-5s; Cloud (Haiku) — ~$0.002, ~3s; Cloud Free (Gemma 4 31B) — $0.00.
notes.note-2: AI section now shows provider badge and 🔵 Embedded indicator when vector stored in Qdrant.
notes.note-3: Cloud Free radio has model selector dropdown populated from OPENROUTER_MODELS registry. Selection stored in _provider_state.openrouter_model.

DF-001.NAME: Employee Report (instant)
DF-001.step-1: Endpoint callable via curl for external integrations only

DF-002.NAME: Session Summarize (on-demand, from detail modal)
DF-002.TRIGGER: User clicks '🧠 Анализ сессии целиком' sub-button in detail modal
DF-002.step-2: M-SUMMARY-STORE checks cache — if hit, return immediately
DF-002.step-3: Cache miss: load session generations from DB
DF-002.step-4: BLOCK_FETCH_LOGS: for each generation, call Polza.AI /history/generations/{id}/log
DF-002.step-5: BLOCK_CALL_LLM: Anthropic native call with cache_control system block
DF-002.step-8: Return JSON; frontend re-renders session sub-block with topic + summary

DF-005.NAME: Per-generation AI analysis (on-demand, inside detail modal)
DF-005.TRIGGER: User opens detail modal and clicks '🧠 Проанализировать'
DF-005.step-1: Frontend openDetail(genId) renders modal — AI block shows empty state with cost disclosure "$0.002"
DF-005.step-2: User clicks button → runGenAnalysis(genId) posts to /api/generation/summarize {generationId}
DF-005.step-3: BLOCK_GEN_CACHE_CHECK: gen_summary_get_or_none — if hit, return cached dict with cached:true
DF-005.step-5: BLOCK_GEN_CALL_LLM: _llm_call_summarize — Anthropic native call with cache_control
DF-005.step-7: BLOCK_GEN_CACHE_STORE: gen_summary_upsert with full usage metrics
DF-005.step-8: Return JSON including cacheCreationTokens / cacheReadTokens / inputTokens / outputTokens / llmCost
DF-005.step-9: Frontend re-renders AI block with topic, summary, flags, session sub-block, meta line
DF-005.step-10: Table cell is updated via renderTable() — chip with topic replaces grey 🧠
DF-005.evidence: [GenSummarize][LLM] ok input=N output=N cost=$0.00XXXX + [GenSummaryStore][upsert] cached generation_id=...

DF-006.NAME: Summary prefetch on table load
DF-006.step-4: Frontend populates S.summary_{id} and calls renderTable()
DF-006.step-5: AI cells now show chip+topic where cache exists, grey 🧠 elsewhere
DF-006.step-6: Zero LLM calls, only DB read — fast and free

DF-007.NAME: Bulk analyze group
DF-007.TRIGGER: User clicks '🧠 Проанализировать (N)' in '🤖 Без AI-анализа' topic group
DF-007.step-1: bulkAnalyze(btnEl, ids) parses pipe-separated ID list
DF-007.step-3: Each worker pulls next ID, calls POST /api/generation/summarize, updates S[`summary_${id}`]
DF-007.step-4: Progress shown in button text: ⏳ done/total, updated after each completion
DF-007.step-5: Final state: ✅ Готово (N/N); renderTable() to refresh all affected rows
DF-007.evidence: N × [GenSummarize] ok + /api/generation/summarize HTTP/1.1 200 repeated in access log

DF-008.NAME: Provider config + switch
DF-008.TRIGGER: Page load / user clicks provider radio in sidebar
DF-008.step-1: Page load: loadProviderConfig() calls GET /api/provider/config — returns {provider, providers: [{id, name, model, cost_info}]}
DF-008.step-2: Frontend populates radio state and info labels (model name, cost estimate)
DF-008.step-3: User clicks alternative provider → setProvider() POST /api/provider/set {provider: "ollama"|"anthropic"}
DF-008.step-4: Backend updates _provider_state dict — takes effect on next _llm_call_summarize dispatch
DF-008.step-5: Response confirms new provider; frontend updates labels and badge
DF-008.evidence: [Provider] switched to ollama/anthropic + GET /api/provider/config returns updated state

DF-009.NAME: Parallel LLM + Embed + dual store
DF-009.TRIGGER: User clicks '🧠 Проанализировать' with any provider active
DF-009.step-1: Cache miss path: ThreadPoolExecutor submits two futures in parallel
DF-009.step-2: Future-A: _llm_call_summarize dispatches to _llm_call_anthropic() or _llm_call_ollama() based on _provider_state
DF-009.step-3: Future-B: _embed_text() calls Ollama /api/embed with nomic-embed-text-v2-moe — 768-dim vector
DF-009.step-4: Both futures complete → parse LLM JSON via _parse_llm_json
DF-009.step-6: Store in Qdrant via _qdrant_upsert (Polza_user_logs) — payload: generation_id, topic, session_id, key_name, model, created_at
DF-009.step-7: Return JSON to frontend — includes provider badge info

DF-010.NAME: Persistent Analyze-All
DF-010.TRIGGER: User clicks '🧠 Анализ всех' button OR server resumes from DB state
DF-010.step-1: POST /api/analyze-all/start — sets analysis_state status=running, starts background thread
DF-010.step-2: Worker queries Generation.created_at_api desc, filters cached via gen_summary_get_many
DF-010.step-3: For each uncached: _analyze_single_gen (LLM + embed + dual store), update analysis_state every 5 records
DF-010.step-4: GET /api/analysis-stats returns live {total, analyzed, remaining, job: {status, done, errors, startedAt}}
DF-010.step-5: Frontend polls every 2s — renders stats counter, progress banner with ETA, pause/resume/stop buttons
DF-010.step-6: Stop → analysis_state.status=paused. Start again → detects paused state, resumes from where left off
DF-010.step-7: Server restart → main() reads analysis_state, if status=running|paused → auto-resume background thread
DF-010.evidence: [AnalyzeAll] N remaining (of M total) + [AnalysisState] update + GET /api/analysis-stats 200

DF-011.NAME: Auto-Analyze on Sync
DF-011.TRIGGER: SyncWorker completes sync cycle with new records AND auto_analyze toggle is ON
DF-011.step-2: If new_ids not empty AND self.on_new_records callback set → callback(new_ids)
DF-011.step-3: _auto_analyze_new_records: check _provider_state.auto_analyze, check analyze-all not already running
DF-011.step-4: Filter already-analyzed via gen_summary_get_many, call _analyze_single_gen for each uncached
DF-011.step-5: Each record: LLM + embed + dual store (PostgreSQL + Qdrant). 0.5s delay between calls.
DF-011.evidence: [AutoAnalyze] N new records to auto-analyze + [AutoAnalyze] ✅ gen_id...

DF-012.NAME: OpenRouter provider
DF-012.TRIGGER: User selects Cloud Free radio or auto-analyze with openrouter active
DF-012.step-2: OpenAI-compatible POST to /v1/chat/completions with Bearer auth

Phase-1.name: Employee Report (no AI)
Phase-1.goal: Fast aggregated employee view — instant value, zero AI cost, pure SQL
Phase-1.step-4: UI tab added then removed in simplify-ui refactor

Phase-2.name: Session Summarizer (LLM)
Phase-2.goal: AI-powered session analysis via Polza.AI → then migrated to Anthropic native
Phase-2.step-2: Background summarize-all thread — endpoints remain, UI removed

Phase-3.name: AI per-generation + UX refactor
Phase-3.goal: Manual-trigger per-generation AI analysis inside detail modal, topic-based grouping, bulk analyze
Phase-3.step-1: GenerationSummary model + full CRUD including batch fetch
Phase-3.step-2: Direct Anthropic Messages API integration with cache_control, cost accounting, error mapping
Phase-3.step-3: Endpoints: POST summarize, POST batch, DELETE cache. Extended prompt for 3-5 sentence output.
Phase-3.step-4: UI refactor: AI section inside detail modal, compact ai-cell, topic tab, bulk analyze, session sub-block
Phase-3.step-5: Deploy to VM: /home/user/POLZA (consolidated from POLZA.AI + POLZA-new), branch main

Phase-4.name: Qwen On-Prem + Vector Embeddings
Phase-4.goal: Dual LLM provider (Anthropic Cloud + Ollama On-Prem), embedding pipeline via nomic-embed-text-v2-moe, Qdrant vector storage, provider toggle UI
Phase-4.step-1: Dual provider abstraction — _llm_call_anthropic, _llm_call_ollama, _llm_call_summarize dispatcher, _parse_llm_json, provider config/set endpoints
Phase-4.step-2: Embedding pipeline via Ollama /api/embed — nomic-embed-text-v2-moe, 768-dim vectors
Phase-4.step-3: Qdrant client wrapper — lazy init, collection ensure, upsert with payload (polza-qdrant on port 6335)
Phase-4.step-4: Refactored: parallel LLM + embed via ThreadPoolExecutor, dual store (PostgreSQL + Qdrant)
Phase-4.step-5: Sidebar provider toggle — radio buttons, info labels, provider badge in AI section
Phase-4.step-6: New endpoints: GET /api/provider/config, POST /api/provider/set. New env vars: LLM_PROVIDER, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL, OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL, OLLAMA_THINKING, OLLAMA_TIMEOUT, QDRANT_URL, QDRANT_COLLECTION, QDRANT_ENABLED, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_MODEL, OPENROUTER_BASE_URL
Phase-4.step-7: Provider toggle in sidebar, updated renderAiSection with provider label and vector badge

Phase-4b.name: Persistent Analyze-All + Auto-Analyze + UX Polish
Phase-4b.goal: Make analyze-all persistent (survives restart), implement auto-analyze for new records, configurable refresh interval, visual analysis status indicators, always-visible stats counter
Phase-4b.step-1: AnalysisState singleton table in DB — persists analyze-all job progress (status, total, done, errors, timestamps)
Phase-4b.step-2: sync_key returns (count, new_ids, error). SyncWorker.on_new_records callback for auto-analyze. sync_all_keys returns (results, all_new_ids).
Phase-4b.step-3: Auto-analyze callback: SyncWorker → _auto_analyze_new_records → filters cached → _analyze_single_gen for each. Toggle via AUTO_ANALYZE env + /api/provider/set.
Phase-4b.step-4: GET /api/analysis-stats (always-available counts + job state). Analyze-all state persisted to DB. Server startup auto-resumes. Stop = pause.
Phase-4b.step-5: Configurable refresh interval (10s-5min dropdown). Persistent stats counter in topbar. Visual status: 🔄 В очереди / ⏳ Ожидает / 🧠 / ✅. Auto-resume polling on page load.
Phase-4b.step-6: New AnalysisState model + get_analysis_state, update_analysis_state, get_analysis_counts helpers. New analysis_state table.

Phase-5.name: Modular Refactoring
Phase-5.goal: Split monolithic polza_dashboard.py (2168 lines) into modular packages for maintainability. Zero functional changes — all endpoints and behavior preserved.
Phase-5.step-1: Extract config.py: Flask app factory, global state, env loading, shared helpers
Phase-5.step-2: Extract providers/: anthropic.py, ollama.py, openrouter.py, prompt.py, dispatcher.py with __init__.py re-exports
Phase-5.step-3: Extract embeddings/: embed.py (_embed_text, _extract_user_text_from_log), qdrant.py (client, collection, upsert)
Phase-5.step-5: Extract routes/: misc.py, generations.py, summarize.py, sessions.py, analyze.py, employee.py, provider.py, keys.py, sync.py, proxy.py with register_all(app) in __init__.py
Phase-5.step-6: Reduce polza_dashboard.py to entrypoint: main() only (148 lines)

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
